_comment: NPC: Wang Tianshan (王天山). Canon ID: N04. Per user directive: every miscellaneous NPC from RI must be accounted for. Prime Directive: Reality is objective; cultivation changes understanding, not existence. Every being described here exists independently of the player's perception.
name: Wang Tianshan

dao_heart.note: 0-100. A cracked Dao heart blocks breakthrough regardless of Qi. Per canon: Wang Lin's Dao heart is famously tested at every major realm.

initiation_lines: Young one, respect your elders. The Wang family's reputation was built on discipline, not luck.
When I was your age, the Teng family was just a merchant clan. Now they act like nobility. Times change.
The memorial shrine must be maintained. Our ancestors watch over us. Do not forget that.

sect_tasks.description: Wang Tianshan gestures toward the memorial shrine: 'The ancestors watch over us, but the incense burns low. Bring coal for the braziers — our forefathers deserve warmth, even in death.'
Wang Tianshan frowns: 'Bandits have been seen on the northern road. I am too old to patrol as I once did. Bring arrows — the village watch needs them.'

desires.what: Express concern about the village's decline to a family member
desires.why: The Wang family has been weakening for years. As elder, it is my duty to remind the younger generation of what we are losing.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1-3. The village is in visible decline under Teng family pressure. Canon basis: village poverty and Teng oppression are central to Wang Lin's early motivation. An elder who remembers better days would comment on this.
desires.line: Fifty years ago, this village had three hundred people. Now look at us. The Teng family takes our grain, our young men leave for the city, and the spirit vein... I do not know what is wrong with the spirit vein.

desires.what: Ask if anyone has noticed strangers or bandits near the village
desires.why: The northern road has had bandit sightings. The village watch needs information to prepare.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1. Bandits are mentioned in the village context. The sect_task 'village_patrol_arrows' confirms a bandit threat. An elder responsible for safety would actively gather intelligence from anyone who travels outside the village.
desires.line: Have you seen anyone unfamiliar on the northern road? I have heard reports of bandits. The village must know before it is too late.

desires.what: Remind family members to maintain the memorial shrine
desires.why: The ancestors built this village. Neglecting the shrine weakens the family's spirit and invites misfortune.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1. Chinese village culture emphasizes ancestor worship. The shrine exists in the village per the sect_task data. An elder would remind people to tend it — this is universal elder behavior in a traditional village.
desires.line: The shrine braziers are burning low again. Our ancestors watch over us. Someone bring coal before the evening ceremony. We cannot afford their displeasure on top of everything else.

desires.what: Mention that the village's spirit vein has been weakening
desires.why: The crops have been poor for three seasons. The spirit vein feeds the land. If it fails, the village dies.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1. Wang Family Village sits on a spirit vein (canon). Its weakening explains the village's decline and connects to the broader cultivation world. The elder would be the one to notice and speak of this. Per Art XXXI.4 Ecology/Gold-Standard dimension 1.
desires.line: The spirit vein beneath this village grows thinner each year. I can feel it. The herbs in the eastern field used to grow tall. Now they barely sprout. If this continues, we will have to abandon this place within a generation.
